message:
perf(storage): unify message-fingerprint lookups + append-only tail fast path

Two coordinated changes to the FrankenStorage merge path that both aim
to reduce database round-trips when ingesting incremental conversation
updates.

1. Single-pass `franken_existing_message_lookup`
------------------------------------------------
Before: every existing-conversation merge issued two separate queries
against the `messages` table — `franken_existing_message_fingerprints_by_idx`
to build a `HashMap<idx, MessageMergeFingerprint>`, then
`franken_existing_message_replay_fingerprints` to build a
`HashSet<MessageReplayFingerprint>`. Each query did its own row decode
of (role, author, created_at, content) and a blake3 hash.

Now: one `franken_existing_message_lookup(tx, conv_id, incoming)` call
returns a new `ExistingMessageLookup { by_idx, replay }` populated in
a single pass:
- The SELECT pulls `idx` in addition to the role/author/created_at/content
  columns.
- When the incoming batch lets us bound the scan, the WHERE clause is
  the union of `(idx BETWEEN min_idx AND max_idx)` and the existing
  created_at-window predicate, so the same rows that drove the old
  by-idx query and the same rows that drove the old replay query are
  both returned by one execution.
- We blake3-hash the content once per row and route it into the
  `by_idx` map (when the row's idx is in the incoming range) and into
  the `replay` set (when its created_at falls in the window, or always
  on the full-scan path).

All four call sites that previously called the two helpers now call
the unified helper:
- `merge_conversation`'s ConversationInsertStatus::Existing arm
- the profiled merge path (also adds an `existing_idx_lookup_duration`
  / `existing_replay_lookup_duration` consolidation)
- the bulk merge in `merge_conversations` — wrapped in
  `franken_existing_message_lookup_with_pending` so the existing
  pending-fingerprint caches still work
- a fourth merge variant in the same impl block

`franken_existing_message_replay_fingerprints` is removed; its
behavior is now part of the unified helper.

2. Append-only tail fast path
-----------------------------
For the very common "agent ran one more turn and we're re-ingesting
the same conversation" pattern, the fingerprint lookup is wasteful —
the new messages strictly extend past the existing tail, so we don't
need to load anything to dedupe.

New `franken_existing_conversation_append_tail_state(tx, conv_id)`
issues one cheap query — `SELECT MAX(idx), MAX(created_at) FROM
messages WHERE conversation_id = ?` — and returns the existing tail
position.

New `collect_append_only_tail_messages(conv, max_idx, max_created_at)`
walks the incoming `conv.messages` and returns
`Some(ExistingConversationNewMessages)` only if:
- messages are non-decreasing on idx (so we can find a contiguous tail)
- the first message past `max_idx` is the split point
- every tail message has a `created_at` strictly greater than
  `max_created_at`
- tail idx values and replay fingerprints are unique within the tail

If any of those preconditions fail we return None and the profiled
path falls back to the full unified lookup. When the fast path hits,
it returns the same ExistingConversationNewMessages shape the slow
path produces, so the surrounding insert/update code is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
